item.title: Buttered Noodles
item.description: Ingredients 1 bag of the widest egg noodles you can find 1 stick of butter, unsalted or salted, doesn’t matter fresh garlic or garlic powder black pepper salt Directions Put water and a decent amount of salt in a pot and bring it to a boil. Add noodles and cook till al dente, or about a minute shy of the package directions. Drain noodles quickly and return to warm pot.
item.content:
Ingredients

  • 1 bag of the widest egg noodles you can find
  • 1 stick of butter, unsalted or salted, doesn’t matter
  • fresh garlic or garlic powder
  • black pepper
  • salt

Directions

  1. Put water and a decent amount of salt in a pot and bring it to a boil.
  2. Add noodles and cook till al dente, or about a minute shy of the package directions.
  3. Drain noodles quickly and return to warm pot.
  4. Add butter and cover with noodles so it will melt faster.
  5. Add garlic and black pepper.
  6. Stir well and make sure the butter melts all the way.
  7. Add salt if needed.

item.title: Gnocchi, Sausage and Peas
item.description: Ingredients 1 lb package gnocchi 10 oz. frozen peas 1 lb mild Italian sausage 3/4 cup grated parmesan 3 teaspoons lemon juice Directions Fill a medium pot half way with water. Salt the water generously and bring to a boil While waiting for the water to boil brown the sausage in a 12 inch skillet. When the sausage is almost done, add the gnocchi to the water. Make sure to break up any chunks so they don’t stick together.
item.content:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb package gnocchi
  • 10 oz. frozen peas
  • 1 lb mild Italian sausage
  • 3/4 cup grated parmesan
  • 3 teaspoons lemon juice

Directions

  1. Fill a medium pot half way with water. Salt the water generously and bring to a boil
  2. While waiting for the water to boil brown the sausage in a 12 inch skillet.
  3. When the sausage is almost done, add the gnocchi to the water. Make sure to break up any chunks so they don’t stick together.
  4. Reserve a cup or two of the pasta water and drain the gnocchi when they float.
  5. Add gnocchi to the sausage skillet and mix together.
  6. Add peas and some of the pasta water. Mix throughly.
  7. Add parmesan cheese and lemon juice.
  8. Continue mixing until the cheese melts and the resulting sauce is creamy.
  9. Add salt and pepper if needed.
